name: Circular route from Calpe to Serra de Bernia
name_original: Vuelta circular desde Calpe hasta la Serra de Bernia
region_intro: Road cycling around Calp, located on Spain's Costa Blanca, offers diverse terrain ranging from flat coastal routes to challenging mountain passes. The region is characterized by its rugged mountains, limestone formations like the Peñón de Ifach, and scenic valleys. Cyclists can navigate winding roads through pine forests and along the Mediterranean coastline, encountering significant climbs such as Coll de Rates, Sierra de Bernia, and Puerto de Tudons. This varied landscape provides options for different skill levels, from coastal rides to demanding ascents. Best road cycling routes around Calp The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Bèrnia and Cumbre del Sol Loop, a difficult 48.9 miles (78.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Circular route from Calpe to Serra de Bernia, a difficult 35.1 miles (56.4 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and panoramic views of the Serra de Bernia. Local road cyclists also love the Port de Tudons – Guadalest Castle loop from Calp, a 68.3 miles (110.0 km) trail leading through the Port de Tudons pass and towards Guadalest Castle, often completed in about 5 hours 58 minutes. Road cycling around Calp is defined by challenging mountain passes, scenic coastal roads, and routes through traditional Spanish villages. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate distances to long-distance routes with substantial elevation. The routes in Calp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 40,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Calp?
A: Calp offers a wide selection of road cycling routes. You can find over 70 routes on komoot, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Calp?
A: While Calp is known for its challenging mountain passes, there are options for beginners. You can find 8 easy routes on komoot, often featuring flatter coastal sections or less demanding inland roads.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Calp?
A: Calp boasts a favorable climate with mild winters and abundant sunshine. Spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) offer particularly pleasant temperatures for cycling, making them ideal times to visit.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes near Calp?
A: Yes, Calp is an excellent base for longer rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38241729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Tudons – Guadalest Castle loop from Calp&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 110 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10540151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Castle loop from Calp&lt;/a&gt; extends to over 115 km, both offering significant elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Calp?
A: Calp offers diverse terrain, from flat coastal rides with stunning Mediterranean views to demanding climbs through rugged mountains. You'll encounter winding roads through pine forests, scenic valleys like Llíber and Xaló, and challenging mountain passes such as Coll de Rates, Sierra de Bernia, and Puerto de Tudons.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or attractions along Calp's road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer spectacular views and pass by significant landmarks. You can enjoy views of the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/174441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Ifach summit&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/578398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bèrnia Fort&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the picturesque El Castell de Guadalest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4370856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Ifac Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and Morro de Toix Viewpoint also provide breathtaking panoramas.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Calp?
A: Yes, many routes around Calp are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e892015230/circular-route-from-calpe-to-serra-de-bernia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular route from Calpe to Serra de Bernia&lt;/a&gt;, offering challenging climbs and panoramic views.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Calp?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Road cyclists often praise the diverse and challenging terrain, the stunning coastal and mountain views, and the region's cycling-friendly environment.

Q: Are there any routes that feature the famous mountain passes like Coll de Rates or Sierra de Bernia?
A: Absolutely. Calp is renowned for its challenging mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890692334/sierra-de-bernia-and-cumbre-del-sol-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Bèrnia and Cumbre del Sol Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route featuring significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain, including parts of the Sierra de Bernia. Other routes often incorporate sections of Coll de Rates and Puerto de Tudons.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through charming villages?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Calp wind through or near picturesque Spanish villages such as Benissa, Teulada, Denia, Javea, Moraira, Altea, Guadalest, and Xaló. These villages offer opportunities to explore local culture and find amenities.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling in Calp?
A: The region is known for its generally smooth and quiet roads, especially in the mountainous areas, providing an excellent cycling experience. This makes Calp a preferred destination for many cyclists, including professional teams.

Q: Are there any specific highlights related to the Peñón de Ifach along the routes?
A: While the Peñón de Ifach is a prominent landmark, road cycling routes typically offer views of it rather than directly climbing it. You can find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27112527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bèrnia Pass – View of the Rock of Calpe loop from Calp&lt;/a&gt; that provide stunning vistas of this iconic rock.
tips: If you like challenges you can't miss it! A not-so-popular gem within the Marina Baja, with sustained slopes of 15% and maximums above 20%, you'll have to give it your all to be rewarded with stunning views from the top. This point is the furthest you can go since the paved road ends here.

waypoints.name: Alto de la Sierra de Bernia
